_comment: Three-statement IAM policy pattern for Bedrock InvokeModel with full cross-region inference coverage: (1) standard on-demand invocation via exact model ARN, (2) geographic/regional cross-region inference profile, (3) global cross-region inference (three required ARN forms). Global CRIS requires ALL three statements — missing even one causes AccessDeniedException. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/global-cross-region-inference.html and https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/security_iam_id-based-policy-examples.html

Statement._comment: Standard on-demand invocation. Scoped to a specific foundation model ARN. Do NOT use arn:aws:bedrock:*::foundation-model/* — that grants access to any model. Replace <MODEL_ID> with e.g. anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6 or anthropic.claude-opus-4-6-v1.

Statement 1 of 3 required for Global cross-region inference. References the inference-profile ARN in the source region with aws:RequestedRegion condition. Replace <MODEL_ID> with e.g. anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6 (the global. prefix is used in the resource ARN).
Statement 2 of 3 required for Global cross-region inference. References the foundation model in the source region, constrained to the inference profile ARN.

Statement 3 of 3 required for Global cross-region inference. References the global foundation model ARN (no region, no account — this is intentional). Uses aws:RequestedRegion='unspecified' which is the value AWS sets when a request is globally routed. SCPs must explicitly allow 'unspecified' in their region whitelist or Global CRIS will be blocked.

Minimum permissions for the Bedrock Agents classic service role. Attach this to the role named AmazonBedrockExecutionRoleForAgents_<AGENT_NAME>. Add optional statements (Retrieve, ApplyGuardrail, s3:GetObject for OpenAPI schema) as needed.
Optional: include only if the agent uses a knowledge base.
Optional: include only if the agent has a guardrail attached.
Optional: include only if the agent uses an action group with an OpenAPI schema on S3. The aws:ResourceAccount condition ensures only same-account S3 resources are accessible.
